audit_finding: Report labels 2012; final article was published in 2013. The 19.2% quoted-opportunity result is supported.
Earlier version of C01; must not count as independent replication.

work_or_claim: The betting market over time: overround and surebets in European football
audit_finding: Supports declining overround and increasing quoted opportunities in its historical European-football sample; not modern execution proof.

work_or_claim: Profiting from arbitrage and odds biases of the European football gambling market
audit_finding: Retain, but independently verify sample construction before using profitability claims.

work_or_claim: How efficient is the European football betting market? Evidence from arbitrage and trading strategies
audit_finding: Publication year commonly indexed as 2009, while working versions predate it. Preserve version/date provenance.

work_or_claim: New entry, strategic diversity and efficiency in soccer betting markets
audit_finding: 545 portfolios and roughly half requiring a favourite bet at a position-taking bookmaker are supported; do not rewrite this as exactly half being rejected.
Not a surebet execution study. Use as market-anomaly/limits-to-arbitrage context only.

work_or_claim: Beating the bookies with their own numbers
audit_finding: Not pure surebetting and non-peer-reviewed. Account restrictions are relevant operational evidence but not surebet profitability proof.

work_or_claim: Systematic Positive Expected Returns in the UK Fixed Odds Betting Market
audit_finding: Report omits Rob Simmons and conflates this with the Grant arbitrage title. It is not a surebet paper.
The report claims no studies outside European football, but its own catalogue includes horse racing, Jai Alai and Super Rugby-related arbitrage work.
Football has direct evidence; NBA/NFL/Tennis/Darts ranking is not academically established as surebet frequency or executable-profit ranking.
No cited paper derives these universal thresholds. They must remain experiment parameters, not canonical minimums.
